question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 changes the OSPF hello-interval on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (R3↔R4)
to 2s with dead-interval 8s. R4 stays at the broadcast defaults
(hello 10s, dead 40s). OSPF Hello packets carry the timer values; the
receiving side compares against its locally-configured values and
rejects the Hello when they don't match. The adjacency drops out of
Full as soon as the next Hello arrives.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on R3, the OSPF timers on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R3<->R4 link) were changed to hello-interval 2 / dead-interval 8 while R4 keeps the broadcast defaults (10 / 40).
OSPF requires the hello and dead timers to match between neighbors on a link, so R4 rejects R3's Hellos as incompatible and the two ends cannot maintain the adjacency, halting route exchange across the R3<->R4 link.
Fix: remove the hello-interval and dead-interval overrides on R3's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (restore the 10/40 defaults).
expected_symptoms_summary:
OSPF adjacency state change (%ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG):
- R3: neighbor 192.0.2.4 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "dead timer expired".
- R4: neighbor 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 (area 0) FULL → DOWN reason "dead timer expired".
- Bilateral: both ends (R3, R4) report the transition.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 hello-interval 2
 dead-interval 8

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router ospf 100
area 0
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 no hello-interval
 no dead-interval

expected_symptoms: RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:11:31.484 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.4 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: dead timer expired, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000

RP/0/RP0/CPU0:May 17 14:11:35.694 UTC: ospf[1035]: %ROUTING-OSPF-5-ADJCHG : Process 100, Nbr 192.0.2.3 on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/0 in area 0 from FULL to DOWN, Neighbor Down: dead timer expired, vrf default vrfid 0x60000000
